preamble: The correspondence between the voiced labiodental fricative [v] (or its bilabial stop reflex [b]) and the lateral [l] is a documented phonetic shift in the Nakh-Dagestani language family, where laboratory sounds in one dialect correspond to liquids in another.
phoneticEffects: Liquid Correspondence, Consonantalization Shift, Dialectal Correspondence

examples.note: Regular correspondence where the Bats labial consonant corresponds to a lateral sound in Chechen.

sources: Phonological studies on Nakh language correspondences.
